layman_headline: Extreme Fear grips the market — no clean swing trade setup stands out right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is deep in 'Extreme Fear' territory at 25/100 and Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.8%, which means altcoins are getting crushed relative to BTC. Every swing candidate on the watchlist has significant problems: some have already rallied hard and are extended (ONDO +26%, LDO +19%), others are in strong downtrends with no signs of bottoming (BONK), and a few are too illiquid or obscure to trade safely. The data is telling us to be patient and wait for a cleaner setup rather than force a trade.
layman_action: Stay in cash. The market is fearful and altcoins are weak. Wait for a clear reversal signal or a quality coin to pull back to strong support before committing capital.
expert_reasoning: Systematic review of all 9 candidates reveals no high-conviction entry. BONK has the most oversold RSI (31) but the chart bias is -2.0 (strongly bearish) with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu — classic capitulation patterns where catching the knife is dangerous. LDO has the best chart bias (+0.85) with Three White Soldiers, but RSI at 59 after a +51.7% 5-day move and a poor R/R ratio of 0.38x means the easy money has been made. ONDO is similarly extended (+26% in 7 days) with 0.38x R/R. BinanceLife has a bull flag pattern but is too obscure with unknown liquidity. DEXE shows a massive data discrepancy (price listed at $8.18 but chart says $36.27, -77% 24h move) suggesting a structural event like a token split or exploit — untouchable. ETH is the safest large-cap play but the chart is bearish (-0.7) with price below all MAs. In Extreme Fear with 56.8% BTC dominance, capital preservation trumps forced entries.
risk_factors: Extreme Fear can persist for weeks, dragging altcoins lower even if BTC holds steady
BTC dominance at 56.8% signals continued capital rotation away from alts — fighting this trend is risky
No candidate offers risk/reward above 2x with confirmed reversal signals
watch_list: BTC price action at $68,000 resistance — a clean breakout or rejection sets the tone for altcoins
ETH reclaiming its 20-day SMA — would flip the chart from bearish to neutral and open a swing entry
Fear & Greed index recovering above 30 — would signal sentiment stabilization and safer alt entries
market_context: The crypto market is in Extreme Fear (25/100) with BTC dominance elevated at 56.8%, creating a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades. BTC itself is showing modest strength (+2.8% weekly) but faces key resistance at $68K.
context_banner: Capital preservation is the priority — Extreme Fear and high BTC dominance make altcoin swing trades low-probability right now.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 25/100 with flat trend suggests sustained risk-off sentiment — not ideal for initiating new altcoin positions.
BONK RSI at 31 is the most oversold candidate, but the -2.0 chart bias with Three Black Crows confirms this is a falling knife, not a bounce setup.
LDO has the strongest chart (+0.85 with Three White Soldiers) but R/R of only 0.38x after +51% in 5 days means the move is already extended.
ETH breaking $1,900 is a positive headline but the chart remains below all MAs — the breakout needs confirmation before trading it.
BTC facing key test at $68K and Movement Labs bankruptcy highlight mixed macro backdrop with both opportunity and risk.
